Rung.Comment: Get data from Adept Controller
If configuration checking is disabled, set the allow config change bit
Main Permissive speed safety governing is built in, just check accel

****************** Location Download Section ******************
Robot_Command.cmd_location_define downloads the Robot_Command.Location coordinates to the Adept controller memory. Locations must be downloaded each time the Adept controller is restarted (indicated by the Robot_Status.system_initialized_state bit becoming true).

Adept controller has just booted, reset variables so all locations will be downloaded. Locations can also be downloaded by issuing download request Pallets will automatically be downloaded after locations.
Enable Location Download
Location #0 cannot be defined, start at 1.
Put coordinate data and location number into the command registers and issue define location command.

Since both the define location command bit and the execution bit are on, then increment the counter to download the next location. The preset should be set to the location number + 1 you wish to download.

****************** Pallet Download Section ******************
Robot_Command.cmd_pallet_define downloads the pallet definitions to the Adept controller memory. Pallets must be downloaded each time the Adept controller is restarted (indicated by the Robot_Status.system_initialized_state bit becoming true).
Enable Pallet Download, Locations should be downloaded first.
Pallet #0 cannot be defined, start at 1.
Put pallet data and number into the command registers and issue define pallet command.
Since both the define pallet command bit and the execution bit are on, then increment the counter to download the next pallet. The preset should be set to the pallet number + 1 you wish to download.

Rung.Text: XIC(Robot_Commands.Teach)[XIO(TeachConfigFeedback.Coordinate_in_Joints) COP(Adept_Status_UDT.Here,TeachConfigFeedback.Coordinate,1) ,XIC(TeachConfigFeedback.Coordinate_in_Joints) COP(Adept_Status_UDT.PHere,TeachConfigFeedback.Coordinate,1) ,XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.righty_configuration) OTL(TeachConfigFeedback.Righty) ,XIO(Adept_Status_UDT.righty_configuration) OTU(TeachConfigFeedback.Righty) ,XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.below_configuration) OTL(TeachConfigFeedback.Below) ,XIO(Adept_Status_UDT.below_configuration) OTU(TeachConfigFeedback.Below) ,XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.flip_configuration) OTL(TeachConfigFeedback.Flip) ,XIO(Adept_Status_UDT.flip_configuration) OTU(TeachConfigFeedback.Flip) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach) ];

Rung.Comment:
Copy the current location and edited config data to the Location tag.
Reset the location download bit to force the locations to be downloaded.
Move To function
inc pos Move To function
Rung.Text: XIC(Robot_Commands.Teach_IncPosMoveTo)[XIO(bitDownloading) EQU(Tool_Step,0) EQU(statManualControl,16) XIC(Internal_Permissive_to_Run_Robot) [COP(LocationsConfig[TeachIndex],LocationsConfig[0],1) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis1_Selected) MOV(Robot_Commands.IncMoveDistance,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.X) ,XIO(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis1_Selected) MOV(0,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.X) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis2_Selected) MOV(Robot_Commands.IncMoveDistance,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Y) ,XIO(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis2_Selected) MOV(0,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Y) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis3_Selected) MOV(Robot_Commands.IncMoveDistance,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Z) ,XIO(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis3_Selected) MOV(0,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Z) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis4_Selected) MOV(Robot_Commands.IncMoveDistance,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Yaw) ,XIO(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis4_Selected) MOV(0,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Yaw) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis5_Selected) MOV(Robot_Commands.IncMoveDistance,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Pitch) ,XIO(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis5_Selected) MOV(0,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Pitch) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis6_Selected) MOV(Robot_Commands.IncMoveDistance,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Roll) ,XIO(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis6_Selected) MOV(0,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Roll) ,MOV(0,Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex) ,MOV(0,Robot_Commands.Approach_Height) ,OTL(Robot_Commands.IncMove_Req) ] ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_IncPosMoveTo) ];

Rung.Comment: inc neg Move To function
Rung.Text: XIC(Robot_Commands.Teach_IncNegMoveTo)[XIO(bitDownloading) EQU(Tool_Step,0) EQU(statManualControl,16) XIC(Internal_Permissive_to_Run_Robot) [COP(LocationsConfig[TeachIndex],LocationsConfig[0],1) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis1_Selected) NEG(Robot_Commands.IncMoveDistance,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.X) ,XIO(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis1_Selected) MOV(0,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.X) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis2_Selected) NEG(Robot_Commands.IncMoveDistance,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Y) ,XIO(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis2_Selected) MOV(0,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Y) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis3_Selected) NEG(Robot_Commands.IncMoveDistance,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Z) ,XIO(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis3_Selected) MOV(0,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Z) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis4_Selected) NEG(Robot_Commands.IncMoveDistance,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Yaw) ,XIO(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis4_Selected) MOV(0,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Yaw) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis5_Selected) NEG(Robot_Commands.IncMoveDistance,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Pitch) ,XIO(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis5_Selected) MOV(0,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Pitch) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis6_Selected) NEG(Robot_Commands.IncMoveDistance,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Roll) ,XIO(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis6_Selected) MOV(0,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Roll) ,MOV(0,Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex) ,MOV(0,Robot_Commands.Approach_Height) ,OTL(Robot_Commands.IncMove_Req) ] ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_IncNegMoveTo) ];

Rung.Comment: Feedback to HMI
Unlock editing when index is changed

Robot_Command.Acceleration_profile selects the acceleration profile that will be used to start and end the motion. When set to zero, the trapezoidal profile is used The trapezoidal profile consists of a constant acceleration to the steady-state transit speed, followed by a constant deceleration to the motion endpoint. When set to a value greater than zero, the S-curve profile is used. The S-curve profile uses soft transitions between each element. It consists of: stopped to acceleration ramp; acceleration ramp to steady-state transit speed; steady-state transit speed to deceleration ramp; and deceleration to motion end point.
Note: Jump ignores approach height, Move with approach height is just a Z offset, so one has to first move to location with Z offset, then move to desired position

Robot_Command.cmd_high_power attempts to enable robot high-power, if the system is not in a fault state. If faulted, this bit will have no effect. This bit must be latched on for high-power to be maintained.
If brake commanded when jumping, trigger another brake request to stop the queued move after the inital move is stopped.
Robot_Command.cmd_brake cancels the current motion and decelerates the robot to a stop. If another motion is queued when the brake command is given, that motion begins immediately.
Robot_Command.cmd_reset resets the ePLC Connect software if it is in a fault state. The current error message and error number registers are cleared by this reset command.
Robot_Command.cmd_calibrate requests calibration of the robot if the system is not faulted and if robot high power is on.
Robot_Command.cmd_align will move the robot so that the tool Z is aligned with the nearest world axis.
Apply offset to robot locations with tool applied NOTE: ONLY USE FOR A CANTILEVERED EOAT, NOT THE BEST FOR VISION REG
Clear offsets and apply null tool
Send commands to Robot & Wait for Handshake bit on
Wait for Handshake bit off

Rung.Text: [XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode.0) ONS(bitONS.16) MOV(1,Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode.1) ONS(bitONS.17) MOV(2,Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode.2) ONS(bitONS.18) MOV(4,Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode.3) ONS(bitONS.19) MOV(8,Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode.4) ONS(bitONS.20) MOV(16,Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode) ];

Rung.Comment:
Robot_Command.cmd_jog allows the user to move the robot like a pendant.
jog_world_mode: Enable World mode Bit on: Jog axis in World mode jog_tool_mode: Enable Tool mode Bit on: Jog axis in Tool mode jog_joint_mode: Enable Joint mode Bit on: Jog axis in Joint mode jog_free_mode: Enable Free mode Bit on: Put axis in Free mode
Only one of the jog-mode bits (bits 0-3) can be on at the same time. When World, Tool, or Joint mode is selected, only one joint or axis bit (bits 5-10) can be selected. In Free mode, multiple joints or axes can be selected.
jog_joint_1_or_x_axis: Joint 1/X-axis Bit on: Select joint 1 or X-axis for jogging jog_joint_2_or_y_axis: Joint 2/Y-axis Bit on: Select joint 2 or Y-axis for jogging jog_joint_3_or_z_axis: Joint 3/Z-axis Bit on: Select joint 3 or Z-axis for jogging jog_joint_4_or_yaw_angle: Joint 4/Yaw-angle Bit on: Select joint 4/Yaw-angle for jogging jog_joint_5_or_pitch_angle: Joint 5/Pitch-angle Bit on: Select joint 5/Pitch-angle for jogging jog_joint_6_or_roll_angle: Joint 6/Roll-angle Bit on: Select joint 6/Roll-angle for jogging
* 5/8/2013 J.Moran added bitAutoJog in parallel with manualmode to allow jogging when not in manual mode
Rung.Text: [XIC(ManualMode) ,XIC(bitAutoJog) ]XIC(Internal_Permissive_to_Run_Robot)XIO(bitDownloading)EQU(Tool_Step,0)[[EQU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Speed,1) MOV(2,Robot_Commands.Jog_Speed) ,EQU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode,8) OTE(Adept_Commands_UDT.jog_world_mode) ,EQU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode,4) OTE(Adept_Commands_UDT.jog_tool_mode) ,EQU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode,2) OTE(Adept_Commands_UDT.jog_joint_mode) ,EQU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode,1) OTE(Adept_Commands_UDT.jog_free_mode) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis1_Selected) OTE(Adept_Commands_UDT.jog_joint_1_or_x_axis) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis2_Selected) OTE(Adept_Commands_UDT.jog_joint_2_or_y_axis) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis3_Selected) OTE(Adept_Commands_UDT.jog_joint_3_or_z_axis) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis4_Selected) OTE(Adept_Commands_UDT.jog_joint_4_or_yaw_angle) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis5_Selected) OTE(Adept_Commands_UDT.jog_joint_5_or_pitch_angle) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis6_Selected) OTE(Adept_Commands_UDT.jog_joint_6_or_roll_angle) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Pos_Req) MOV(Robot_Commands.Jog_Speed,Adept_Commands_UDT.Speed) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Neg_Req) NEG(Robot_Commands.Jog_Speed,Adept_Commands_UDT.Speed) ] ,[XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Pos_Req) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Neg_Req) ,XIC(Adept_Commands_UDT.jog_free_mode) ] XIO(Adept_Commands_UDT.cmd_move) XIO(Adept_Commands_UDT.cmd_jump) LIM(Min_Speed,Robot_Commands.Jog_Speed,Maint_Speed) OTE(Adept_Commands_UDT.cmd_jog) ];

XIO(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode.0)[XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis1_Selected) ONS(bitONS.0) [OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis2_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis3_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis4_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis5_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis6_Selected) ] ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis2_Selected) ONS(bitONS.1) [OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis1_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis3_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis4_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis5_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis6_Selected) ] ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis3_Selected) ONS(bitONS.2) [OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis1_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis2_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis4_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis5_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis6_Selected) ] ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis4_Selected) ONS(bitONS.3) [OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis1_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis2_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis3_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis5_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis6_Selected) ] ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis5_Selected) ONS(bitONS.4) [OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis1_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis2_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis3_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis4_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis6_Selected) ] ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis6_Selected) ONS(bitONS.5) [OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis1_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis2_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis3_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis4_Selected) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Axis5_Selected) ] ];

Rung.Comment: Main Status Bits
Rung.Text: [XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.power_state) OTE(statPower) ,[XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.fault_state) ,XIC(Permissive_Lost_During_Move) ] OTE(statFaulted) ,XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.calibrated_state) OTE(statCalibrated) ,XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.system_initialized_state) OTE(statInitialized) ,XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.emergency_stop_state) OTE(statEstopped) ,XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.command_execution_state) OTE(statCommandActive) ,XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.in_position_state) OTE(statInPosition) ,XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.jog_mode_state) OTE(statJogging) ,XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.tool_mode_state) OTE(statToolMode) ,XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.mcp_connected_state) OTE(statMCPConnected) ,XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.righty_configuration) OTE(statRighty) ,XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.below_configuration) OTE(statBelow) ,XIC(Adept_Status_UDT.flip_configuration) OTE(statFlip) ];

Rung.Comment: Current world and joint locations
Error Code and message

Robot State 1 Returns information about the overall robot state
0 Resetting system after robot power has been turned off. 1 A fatal error has occurred and robot power cannot be turned on. 2 Waiting for user to turn on robot power. 3 Robot power was just turned on; initialization is occurring. 4 Indicates that Manual control mode is active 5 A CALIBRATE command or instruction is executing. 6 Not used. 7 Robot is under program control. 8 Robot power is on; robot is not calibrated and cannot be moved.

Robot State 2 Returns information about the current or previous robot motion
0 No motion instructions executed yet. 1 Normal trajectory evaluation is in progress (including normal acceleration, deceleration and segment transitions). 2 Motion stopped at a planned location. 3 Position error is being nulled at unplanned final location. 4 Motion stopped at an unplanned location due to a belt window violation. 5 Decelerating due to a triggered REACTI or BRAKE instruction. 6 Stopped due to a triggered REACTI or BRAKE instruction. 7 Decelerating due to a hardware error, panic button, or estop instruction. 8 Stopped due to a hardware error, panic button, or estop instruction. 9 Decelerating due to a stop-on-force condition. 10 Stopped due to a stop-on-force condition. 11 Nulling at completion of a spin motion. 12 Stopped after completion of a spin instruction.

Robot State 3 Returns information about the current manual control mode
0 Manual mode without selection. 1 Free-joint mode. 2 Individual joint control. 3 World coordinates control. 4 Tool coordinates control. 5 Computer control enabled.
Rung.Text: [MOV(Adept_Status_UDT.state_3,Control_State) ,EQU(Control_State,5) EQU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode,8) MOV(8,statManualControl) ,EQU(Control_State,5) EQU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode,4) MOV(4,statManualControl) ,EQU(Control_State,5) EQU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode,2) MOV(2,statManualControl) ,EQU(Control_State,5) EQU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode,1) MOV(1,statManualControl) ,EQU(Control_State,5) EQU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode,16) MOV(16,statManualControl) ,EQU(Control_State,5) EQU(Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode,0) MOV(16,Robot_Commands.Jog_Mode) ,[LIM(1,Control_State,2) ,EQU(Control_State,4) ] MOV(Control_State,statManualControl) ,EQU(Control_State,3) MOV(8,statManualControl) ];

Rung.Comment: Robot State 10 Returns the percentage of the current motion that has completed
Below Rungs are for Robot Status display on faceplate
Rung.Text: [XIC(statEstopped) MOV(0,Status) ,XIO(statEstopped) XIC(statFaulted) MOV(1,Status) ,XIO(statEstopped) XIO(statFaulted) XIO(statPower) MOV(2,Status) ,XIO(statEstopped) XIO(statFaulted) XIC(statPower) XIO(statCalibrated) MOV(3,Status) ,XIO(statEstopped) XIO(statFaulted) XIC(statPower) XIC(statInitialized) MOV(4,Status) ,XIO(statEstopped) XIO(statFaulted) XIC(statPower) XIC(statCalibrated) XIO(statInitialized) XIC(statJogging) MOV(5,Status) ,XIO(statEstopped) XIO(statFaulted) XIC(statPower) XIC(statCalibrated) XIO(statInitialized) XIO(statJogging) NEQ(Percent_Move_Complete,100) MOV(6,Status) ,XIO(statEstopped) XIO(statFaulted) XIC(statPower) XIC(statCalibrated) XIO(statInitialized) XIO(statJogging) EQU(Percent_Move_Complete,100) MOV(7,Status) ];

Rung.Comment:
****************** Robot Move Functionality ******************
Robot_Command.cmd_move will move the robot.
Step 0: Load Robot_Command.Speed with the motion speed.
Step 5: Load Robot_Command.Acceleration, Robot_Command.Deceleration, and Robot_Command.Acceleration_profile with the motion acceleration parameters.

Step 10: Load Robot_Command.Location_number with the number of the location to be moved to. If this tag has a value of 0, then the values of Robot_Command.Location.X ... Roll are used as the coordinates. If this tag is greater than 0, then the location must be previously defined. Move pallet data to output UDT

The motion qualifier bits are used to define the characteristics of the next motion whenever a move command (Robot_Command.cmd_move) or jump command (Robot_Command.cmd_jump) is given. Any combination of these bits can be set.
Robot_Command.relative_move determines if the motion is to absolute coordinates or relative to the robot's current position. For relative motion, the coordinates of the specified destination location are added to the current position. Thus, this type of motion is relative to absolute Cartesian coordinates, not relative to the tool.
Robot_Command.joint_move determines whether the component values of the location that is referenced by the Location Number register (Robot_Command.Location_number) are Cartesian or joint coordinates.
Robot_Command.approach_at_absolute determines the effect of the approach-height value (Robot_Command.Approach_height), as follows: • If this bit is off, the next motion will approach the location with this Z offset from the defined location. If this bit is on, the next motion moves to the specified destination location using this absolute Z value, based on the World Coordinate System. That is, the Z component of the location definition will be ignored. NOTE: This overrides the setting of bit 0.
Robot_Command.straightline_move determines if the motion will be joint-interpolated or straight-line. If straight-line motion is chosen, it is not possible to change the arm configuration during the motion (see the Robot_Command.righty_configuration, Robot_Command.below_configuation or Robot_Command.flip_configuration bits).
When Robot_Command.nonull is off, continuous-path motion is disabled, and the robot will null at the destination before the next motion is planned. This could increase cycle time. Enabling this bit will allow the ePLC Connect software to blend motions (i.e., round corners between motion segments), assuming that the next motion is commanded in sufficient time before the current motion completes.
Robot_Command.coarse_nulling, which is used only when Robot_Command.nonull is off, determines the nulling tolerances that will apply at the end of the motion. Fine specifies tighter settling than coarse.
Robot_Command.single_turn off allows full rotations of the robot wrist joints (joint 4 on an Adept Cobra robot). Enabling this bit limits rotations of the wrist joints to the range ±180 degrees.
The final three motion qualifier bits determine the arm configuration that is to be achieved during the motion. For the Adept Cobra robot, only Robot_Command.righty_configuration has any effect. The arm configuration cannot be changed during a straight-line motion (see Robot_Command.straightline_move). Note that these bits have no effect if it is moving to joint coordinates (Robot_Command.joint_move is enabled). When a motion is commanded, the speed and acceleration parameters must be greater than 0 (except that a negative speed is valid for a jog-mode motion). Otherwise, an error will be returned. There are no default values. If moving to taught location and the location number is less than 0, an error will occur. Additionally, if you specify a very low motion speed, it may take a long time for the robot to get to the requested position.

Step 15:
Enable/disable the motion qualifier bits (relative_move, joint_move, righty_configuration, etc.) as desired for the motion
relative_move: 0=Absolute motion, 1=Relative motion joint_coordinates: 0=Cartesian coordinates, 1=Joint coordinates straightline_move: 0= Joint motion, 1= Straight-line motion approach_at_absolute: 0= Relative approach height, 1= Absolute approach height nonull: 0= Null after motion, 1= No-null after motion coarse_nulling: 0= Fine nulling tolerances,1= Coarse nulling tolerances single_turn: 0= Multiple-turn wrist, 1= Single-turn wrist righty_configuration: 0= Lefty arm configuration, 1= Righty arm configuration below_configuration: 0= Above arm configuration, 1= Below arm configuration flip_configuration: 0=No-flip arm configuration, 1= Flip arm configuration
Rung.Text: EQU(Move_Step,15)[OTL(bitConfigMatches) ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Relative_Move) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.relative_move) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Relative_Move) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.relative_move) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Coordinate_in_Joints) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.joint_coordinates) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Coordinate_in_Joints) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.joint_coordinates) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Straightline_Move) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.straightline_move) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Straightline_Move) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.straightline_move) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Approach_Absolute) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.approach_at_absolute) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Approach_Absolute) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.approach_at_absolute) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Continuous_Motion) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.nonull) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Continuous_Motion) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.nonull) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Coarse_Nulling) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.coarse_nulling) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Coarse_Nulling) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.coarse_nulling) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Single_Turn) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.single_turn) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Single_Turn) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.single_turn) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Righty) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.righty_configuration) XIC(statRighty) OTU(bitConfigMatches) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Righty) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.righty_configuration) XIO(statRighty) OTU(bitConfigMatches) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Below) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.below_configuation) XIC(statBelow) OTU(bitConfigMatches) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Below) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.below_configuation) XIO(statBelow) OTU(bitConfigMatches) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Flip) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.flip_configuration) XIC(statFlip) OTU(bitConfigMatches) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Flip) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.flip_configuration) XIO(statFlip) OTU(bitConfigMatches) ] ,XIC(bitConfigCheckEnable) XIO(Robot_Commands.AllowConfigChange) XIO(bitConfigMatches) [OTL(ConfigMismatchFault) ,MOV(0,Move_Step) ] ,XIO(ConfigMismatchFault) MOV(20,Move_Step) ];

Rung.Comment:
Step 20:
Enable the bit Robot_Command.cmd_move to start the motion

Step 25:
Wait for the bit Robot_Status.command_execution_state to go high, indicating the motion has started. If it doesn't start, retry the move command.

Step 30:
Disable the Robot_Command.cmd_move bit.

Step 35:
Wait for the Robot_Status.command_execution_state bit to go low, indicating the Move Robot bit has been turned off.

Step 40:
Wait for the Robot_Status.in_position_state bit to go high, indicating the robot is in position.

****************** Robot Single Axis Incremental Move Functionality ******************
Robot_Command.cmd_move will move the robot.
Step 0: Incremental Move - use jog speed.
Step 5: Incremental Move - Acceleration and Deceleration
Step 10: Incremental Move based on axis selected

Step 15:
Enable/disable the motion qualifier bits (relative_move, joint_move, righty_configuration, etc.) as desired for the motion
relative_move: 0=Absolute motion, 1=Relative motion joint_coordinates: 0=Cartesian coordinates, 1=Joint coordinates straightline_move: 0= Joint motion, 1= Straight-line motion approach_at_absolute: 0= Relative approach height, 1= Absolute approach height nonull: 0= Null after motion, 1= No-null after motion coarse_nulling: 0= Fine nulling tolerances,1= Coarse nulling tolerances single_turn: 0= Multiple-turn wrist, 1= Single-turn wrist righty_configuration: 0= Lefty arm configuration, 1= Righty arm configuration below_configuration: 0= Above arm configuration, 1= Below arm configuration flip_configuration: 0=No-flip arm configuration, 1= Flip arm configuration

Step 20:
Enable the bit Robot_Command.cmd_move to start the motion

Step 25:
Wait for the bit Robot_Status.command_execution_state to go high, indicating the motion has started. If it doesn't start, retry the move command.

Step 30:
Disable the Robot_Command.cmd_move bit.

Step 35:
Wait for the Robot_Status.command_execution_state bit to go low, indicating the Move Robot bit has been turned off.

Step 40:
Wait for the Robot_Status.in_position_state bit to go high, indicating the robot is in position.

****************** Robot Jump Functionality ******************
Robot_Command.Jump command will move the robot.
Step 0: 4 Axis: Load Robot_Command.Speed with the motion speed
Step 5: Load Robot_Command.Acceleration, Robot_Command.Deceleration, and Robot_Command.Acceleration_profile with the motion acceleration parameters.

Step 10: Load Robot_Command.Location_number with the number of the location to be moved to. If this tag has a value of 0, then the values of Robot_Command.Location.X ... Roll are used as the coordinates. If this tag is greater than 0, then the location must be previously defined.

Step 15:
Enable/disable the motion qualifier bits (relative_move, joint_move, righty_configuration, etc.) as desired for the motion
relative_move: 0=Absolute motion, 1=Relative motion joint_coordinates: 0=Cartesian coordinates, 1=Joint coordinates straightline_move: 0= Joint motion, 1= Straight-line motion approach_at_absolute: 0= Relative approach height, 1= Absolute approach height nonull: 0= Null after motion, 1= No-null after motion coarse_nulling: 0= Fine nulling tolerances,1= Coarse nulling tolerances single_turn: 0= Multiple-turn wrist, 1= Single-turn wrist righty_configuration: 0= Lefty arm configuration, 1= Righty arm configuration below_configuration: 0= Above arm configuration, 1= Below arm configuration flip_configuration: 0=No-flip arm configuration, 1= Flip arm configuration
Rung.Text: EQU(Jump_Step,15)[OTL(bitConfigMatches) ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Relative_Move) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.relative_move) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Relative_Move) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.relative_move) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Coordinate_in_Joints) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.joint_coordinates) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Coordinate_in_Joints) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.joint_coordinates) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Straightline_Move) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.straightline_move) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Straightline_Move) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.straightline_move) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Approach_Absolute) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.approach_at_absolute) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Approach_Absolute) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.approach_at_absolute) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Continuous_Motion) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.nonull) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Continuous_Motion) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.nonull) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Coarse_Nulling) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.coarse_nulling) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Coarse_Nulling) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.coarse_nulling) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Single_Turn) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.single_turn) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Single_Turn) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.single_turn) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Righty) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.righty_configuration) XIC(statRighty) OTU(bitConfigMatches) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Righty) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.righty_configuration) XIO(statRighty) OTU(bitConfigMatches) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Below) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.below_configuation) XIC(statBelow) OTU(bitConfigMatches) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Below) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.below_configuation) XIO(statBelow) OTU(bitConfigMatches) ] ,[XIO(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Flip) OTU(Adept_Commands_UDT.flip_configuration) XIC(statFlip) OTU(bitConfigMatches) ,XIC(LocationsConfig[Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex].Flip) OTL(Adept_Commands_UDT.flip_configuration) XIO(statFlip) OTU(bitConfigMatches) ] ,XIC(bitConfigCheckEnable) XIO(Robot_Commands.AllowConfigChange) XIO(bitConfigMatches) [OTL(ConfigMismatchFault) ,MOV(0,Jump_Step) ] ,XIO(ConfigMismatchFault) MOV(20,Jump_Step) ];

Rung.Comment:
Step 20:
Enable the bit Robot_Command.cmd_jump (Jump Command) to start the motion.

Step 25:
Wait for the bit Robot_Status.command_execution_state to go high, indicating the motion has started.

Step 30:
Disable the Robot_Command.cmd_jump bit.

Step 35:
Wait for the Robot_Status.command_execution_state bit to go low, indicating the Move Robot bit has been turned off.

Step 40:
Wait for the Robot_Status.in_position_state bit to go high, indicating the robot is in position.
Step 0: 6 Axis: Make sure jump height is greater than current z height. Does not function with Pallets
Set position to absolute jump height
Rung.Text: EQU(Jump_Step,100)XIC(Jump_Next)[COP(TempLocation,LocationsConfig[0],1) ,MOV(statActualPositionWorld.X,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.X) ,MOV(statActualPositionWorld.Y,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Y) ,MOV(JumpHeightAbs,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Z) ,MOV(statActualPositionWorld.Yaw,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Yaw) ,MOV(statActualPositionWorld.Pitch,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Pitch) ,MOV(statActualPositionWorld.Roll,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate.Roll) ,[XIC(statFlip) OTL(LocationsConfig[0].Flip) ,XIO(statFlip) OTU(LocationsConfig[0].Flip) ] ,[XIC(statRighty) OTL(LocationsConfig[0].Righty) ,XIO(statRighty) OTU(LocationsConfig[0].Righty) ] ,[XIC(statBelow) OTL(LocationsConfig[0].Below) ,XIO(statBelow) OTU(LocationsConfig[0].Below) ] ,OTL(LocationsConfig[0].Straightline_Move) ,MOV(0,Robot_Commands.MoveLocationIndex) ,MOV(110,Jump_Step) ,OTU(Jump_Next) ];

Rung.Comment: Command Move
Wait for move complete.
Set position to above location at absolute jump height
Command Move
Wait for move complete.
Set position to final location
Command Move
Wait for move complete.
Reset the move/jump sequence on an error
Error Code and message display on MCP, also locations
Rung.Text: XIO(bitDownloading)[NEQ(Adept_Status_UDT.error_number,0) [MOV(Adept_Status_UDT.error_number,statFaultCode) ,COP(Adept_Status_UDT.error_message,Adept_Commands_UDT.MCP_message,1) ] ,EQU(Adept_Status_UDT.error_number,0) [RTOS(Adept_Status_UDT.Here.X,DummyString) ,LEQ(DummyString.LEN,0) COP(Space_String,Adept_Commands_UDT.MCP_message,1) ,GRT(DummyString.LEN,0) [GRT(DummyString.LEN,8) MID(DummyString,8,1,X_Loc_String) ,LEQ(DummyString.LEN,8) COP(DummyString,X_Loc_String,1) ,CONCAT(X_String,X_Loc_String,Location_String) ,CONCAT(Location_String,Space_String,Location_String) ,RTOS(Adept_Status_UDT.Here.Y,DummyString) ,GRT(DummyString.LEN,8) MID(DummyString,8,1,Y_Loc_String) ,LEQ(DummyString.LEN,8) COP(DummyString,Y_Loc_String,1) ,CONCAT(Location_String,Y_String,Location_String) ,CONCAT(Location_String,Y_Loc_String,Location_String) ,CONCAT(Location_String,Space_String,Location_String) ,RTOS(Adept_Status_UDT.Here.Z,DummyString) ,GRT(DummyString.LEN,8) MID(DummyString,8,1,Z_Loc_String) ,LEQ(DummyString.LEN,8) COP(DummyString,Z_Loc_String,1) ,CONCAT(Location_String,Z_String,Location_String) ,CONCAT(Location_String,Z_Loc_String,Location_String) ,CONCAT(Location_String,Space_String,Location_String) ,RTOS(Adept_Status_UDT.Here.Yaw,DummyString) ,GRT(DummyString.LEN,8) MID(DummyString,8,1,Yaw_Loc_String) ,LEQ(DummyString.LEN,8) COP(DummyString,Yaw_Loc_String,1) ,CONCAT(Location_String,Yaw_String,Location_String) ,CONCAT(Location_String,Yaw_Loc_String,Location_String) ,CONCAT(Location_String,Space_String,Location_String) ,RTOS(Adept_Status_UDT.Here.Pitch,DummyString) ,GRT(DummyString.LEN,8) MID(DummyString,8,1,Pitch_Loc_String) ,LEQ(DummyString.LEN,8) COP(DummyString,Pitch_Loc_String,1) ,CONCAT(Location_String,Pitch_String,Location_String) ,CONCAT(Location_String,Pitch_Loc_String,Location_String) ,CONCAT(Location_String,Space_String,Location_String) ,RTOS(Adept_Status_UDT.Here.Roll,DummyString) ,GRT(DummyString.LEN,8) MID(DummyString,8,1,Roll_Loc_String) ,LEQ(DummyString.LEN,8) COP(DummyString,Roll_Loc_String,1) ,CONCAT(Location_String,Roll_String,Location_String) ,CONCAT(Location_String,Roll_Loc_String,Location_String) ,COP(Location_String,Adept_Commands_UDT.MCP_message,1) ] ] ];

[LIM(33,Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num,0) MOV(1,Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num) ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.0) ONS(bitONS.8) [MOV(0,intTeachPallet) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.1) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.2) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.3) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.4) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.5) ] ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.1) ONS(bitONS.9) [MOV(1,intTeachPallet) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.0) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.2) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.3) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.4) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.5) ,CLR(TeachIndex) ,COP(Null_Location,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate,1) ] ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.2) ONS(bitONS.10) [MOV(2,intTeachPallet) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.0) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.1) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.3) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.4) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.5) ,CLR(TeachIndex) ,COP(Null_Location,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate,1) ] ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.3) ONS(bitONS.11) [MOV(3,intTeachPallet) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.0) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.1) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.2) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.4) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.5) ,CLR(TeachIndex) ,COP(Null_Location,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate,1) ] ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.4) ONS(bitONS.12) [MOV(4,intTeachPallet) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.0) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.1) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.2) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.3) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.5) ,CLR(TeachIndex) ,COP(Null_Location,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate,1) ] ,XIC(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.5) ONS(bitONS.13) [MOV(5,intTeachPallet) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.0) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.1) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.2) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.3) ,OTU(Robot_Commands.Teach_Pallet_Num.4) ,CLR(TeachIndex) ,COP(Null_Location,LocationsConfig[0].Coordinate,1) ] ];

Rung.Comment: Calculate last pallet location
Send data to Adept Controller
